# proxbox-api (LLM context file)

This file is for AI/code assistants. It summarizes the project structure, runtime behavior, API surfaces, and safe edit workflow.

## 1) Project identity

- Name: `proxbox-api`
- Type: FastAPI backend
- Purpose: synchronize and expose infrastructure data between Proxmox and NetBox (devices, VMs, disks, backups, snapshots, cluster data)
- Primary entrypoint: `proxbox_api/main.py` (`app = create_app()`)
- App factory: `proxbox_api/app/factory.py`
- Docs site config: `mkdocs.yml`
- Main human docs: `README.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/`

## 2) Runtime architecture (high level)

- API layer: `proxbox_api/app/*`, `proxbox_api/routes/*`
- Session/dependency layer: `proxbox_api/session/*`, `proxbox_api/dependencies.py`
- Sync services: `proxbox_api/services/sync/*`
- Schemas/enums: `proxbox_api/schemas/*`, `proxbox_api/enum/*`
- Persistence: `proxbox_api/database.py` (SQLite)
- Helpers/utilities: `proxbox_api/utils/*`, `proxbox_api/logger.py`, `proxbox_api/exception.py`, `proxbox_api/cache.py`
- Proxmox codegen: `proxbox_api/proxmox_codegen/*`
- Generated contracts/models: `proxbox_api/generated/proxmox/*`, `proxbox_api/generated/netbox/*`
- Proxmox -> NetBox normalization: `proxbox_api/proxmox_to_netbox/*`

## 3) Startup flow

1. `create_app()` calls `init_database_and_netbox()` from `proxbox_api/app/bootstrap.py`.
2. DB tables are created/opened and NetBox bootstrap client is initialized unless skipped by env var.
3. FastAPI app is created with lifespan hook.
4. Lifespan registers generated Proxmox proxy routes via `register_generated_proxmox_routes`.
5. CORS, exception handlers, root/meta/cache/full-update/websocket routes, and domain routers are mounted.

## 4) Core environment variables

- `PROXBOX_NETBOX_TIMEOUT` (default `120`): NetBox client timeout (seconds)
- `PROXBOX_VM_SYNC_MAX_CONCURRENCY` (default `4`): VM sync create concurrency
- `PROXBOX_FETCH_MAX_CONCURRENCY` (default `8`): fetch concurrency for storage/backup/snapshot flows
- `PROXBOX_NETBOX_WRITE_CONCURRENCY` (default `8` in VM sync path, `4` in task-history/snapshot paths): max concurrent NetBox write operations
- `PROXBOX_PROXMOX_FETCH_CONCURRENCY` (default `8` in most paths, `4` in task-history path): max concurrent Proxmox read operations
- `PROXBOX_RATE_LIMIT` (default `60`): max API requests per minute per IP address
- `PROXBOX_BACKUP_BATCH_SIZE` (default `5`): backup sync batch size to avoid overwhelming NetBox
- `PROXBOX_BACKUP_BATCH_DELAY_MS` (default `200`): delay in milliseconds between backup batches
- `PROXBOX_CORS_EXTRA_ORIGINS` (default empty): extra CORS origins, comma-separated
- `PROXBOX_EXPOSE_INTERNAL_ERRORS` (default unset): expose exception details in HTTP 500 responses
- `PROXBOX_STRICT_STARTUP` (default unset): fail startup if generated Proxmox routes cannot mount
- `PROXBOX_SKIP_NETBOX_BOOTSTRAP` (default unset): skip default NetBox client bootstrap on startup
- `PROXBOX_NETBOX_GET_CACHE_TTL` (default `60.0`): NetBox GET response cache TTL in seconds (0 to disable)
- `PROXBOX_NETBOX_GET_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES` (default `4096`): max cached GET responses by entry count
- `PROXBOX_NETBOX_GET_CACHE_MAX_BYTES` (default `52428800`): max cache size in bytes (50 MB)
- `PROXBOX_DEBUG_CACHE` (default `0`): enable debug-level cache logging

## 5) API surfaces to know

### Utility/root

- `GET /`
- `GET /version`
- `GET /health`
- `GET /cache`
- `GET /cache/metrics`
- `GET /cache/metrics/prometheus`
- `GET /clear-cache`
- `GET /full-update`
- `GET /full-update/stream` (SSE)

### Authentication (`/auth`)

- `GET /auth/bootstrap-status` - Check if initial bootstrap key registration is needed (auth-exempt)
- `POST /auth/register-key` - Register the first API key (auth-exempt, one-time bootstrap)
- `POST /auth/keys` - Create a new API key (requires authentication)
- `GET /auth/keys` - List all API keys (key values redacted)
- `DELETE /auth/keys/{key_id}` - Delete an API key
- `POST /auth/keys/{key_id}/activate` - Re-activate a deactivated key
- `POST /auth/keys/{key_id}/deactivate` - Deactivate an active key

### Admin

- `GET /admin/` - HTML admin dashboard (excluded from OpenAPI)
- `GET /admin/logs` - In-memory log buffer with optional filters for `level`, `limit`, `offset`, `since`, `operation_id`
- `GET /admin/logs/stream` - SSE real-time log stream with filters for `level`, `errors_only`, `operation_id`, `newer_than_id`

### NetBox (`/netbox`)

- Endpoint CRUD (`/netbox/endpoint*`) with singleton behavior
- `GET /netbox/status`
- `GET /netbox/openapi`

### Proxmox (`/proxmox`)

- Endpoint CRUD (`/proxmox/endpoints*`)
- Discovery/session/status endpoints
- Viewer/codegen endpoints (`/proxmox/viewer/*`)
- Runtime-generated live proxy routes:
  - `/proxmox/api2/{version_tag}/*`
  - `/proxmox/api2/*` alias for `latest`

### DCIM (`/dcim`)

- `GET /dcim/devices`
- `GET /dcim/devices/create`
- `GET /dcim/devices/create/stream` (SSE)
- interface creation endpoints

### Virtualization (`/virtualization`)

- Placeholder cluster routes: `/cluster-types/create`, `/clusters/create` (501)
- VM sync/read routes under `/virtualization/virtual-machines/*`
- Important VM-related sync endpoints:
  - `/create`
  - `/create/stream` (SSE)
  - `/backups/create`
  - `/backups/all/create`
  - `/backups/all/create/stream` (SSE)
  - `/snapshots/create`
  - `/snapshots/all/create`
  - `/snapshots/all/create/stream` (SSE)
  - `/storage/create`
  - `/storage/create/stream` (SSE)

### Sync Individual (`/sync/individual`)

Per-object fine-grained sync routes (12 sub-routers): backup, backup-routine, cluster, device, vm, interface, ip, disk, storage, snapshot, task-history, replication. Each supports dry-run and targeted sync by ID.

### Extras (`/extras`)

- `GET /extras/extras/custom-fields/create` - Create or reconcile ~30 Proxmox-related custom fields in NetBox

### WebSockets

- `ws://<host>:<port>/` (counter stream)
- `ws://<host>:<port>/ws/virtual-machines`
- `ws://<host>:<port>/ws` with commands:
  - `Full Update Sync`
  - `Sync Nodes`
  - `Sync Virtual Machines`

## 6) Sync workflow order (full update)

Current full-update pipeline in `proxbox_api/app/full_update.py`:

1. devices
2. storage
3. virtual-machines
4. task-history
5. virtual-disks
6. backups
7. snapshots
8. node-interfaces
9. vm-interfaces
10. vm-ip-addresses
11. replications
12. backup-routines

SSE endpoints use `WebSocketSSEBridge` to convert websocket-style progress payloads into SSE `step/error/complete` frames.

## 7) Generated artifacts and safety

- Generated Proxmox artifacts live under `proxbox_api/generated/proxmox/`.
- Runtime cache manifest: `proxbox_api/generated/proxmox/runtime_generated_routes_cache.json`.
- Prefer editing codegen sources (`proxbox_api/proxmox_codegen/*`) instead of manual edits to generated outputs.
- Keep route/path parameter normalization behavior consistent with generated OpenAPI contracts.

## 8) Repository navigation for edits

- Root guide: `CLAUDE.md`
- Scoped guides: `**/CLAUDE.md` (use nearest guide for the directory being edited)
- User docs (English): `docs/*`
- User docs (pt-BR): `docs/pt-BR/*`

## 9) Development commands

Install:

```bash
uv sync
uv sync --extra test --group dev
uv sync --extra docs --group dev
```

Common checks:

```bash
rtk ruff check .
rtk ruff format --check .
uv run python -m compileall proxbox_api scripts tests
uv run python -c "import proxbox_api.main"
uv run python -c "from proxbox_api.proxmox_to_netbox.proxmox_schema import load_proxmox_generated_openapi; assert load_proxmox_generated_openapi().get('paths')"
rtk pytest tests
uv run mkdocs build --strict
```

## 10) Edit guidelines for LLMs

- Keep route handlers orchestration-focused; move reusable logic to services/helpers.
- Raise `ProxboxException` for predictable domain errors.
- Preserve existing response semantics (notably VM read route 404/502 behavior and 501 stubs).
- Prefer `logger` over `print`.
- Keep normalization/parsing in Pydantic schema layer when adding new transforms.
- Update both EN and pt-BR docs when changing user-facing behavior.
- Do not silently swallow exceptions with broad fallback logic.

## 11) Quick factual references

- Python target: `>=3.11`
- API framework: FastAPI
- Proxmox client: `proxmox-sdk`
- NetBox client facade: `netbox-sdk`
- ORM: SQLModel
- Local DB default file: `database.db`
- Runtime deps (notable): `fastapi[standard]`, `proxmox-sdk`, `netbox-sdk`, `sqlmodel`, `aiosqlite`, `cryptography`, `bcrypt`

End of llms context file.
